questions: You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. You get a call that your parent has been rushed to the hospital while you're at work. How do you react?
You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. Your landlord tells you that you have to move out in two weeks, and you have nowhere to go. What do you do?
You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. Three major deadlines hit simultaneously and your computer crashes, losing unsaved work. How do you respond?

You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. You discover your bank account has been drained by fraudulent charges and your rent is due tomorrow. What happens?

You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. Your child calls you crying from school saying nobody will sit with them and they want to come home. How do you react?
You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. During a presentation to executives, your slides disappear and the backup file is corrupted. What do you do?

You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. You're in a foreign country, your wallet is stolen, your phone is dead, and you don't speak the language. How do you handle it?
You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. You just found out you failed an exam that determines whether you graduate. How do you react?

You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. Multiple family members are fighting at a holiday dinner and everyone is looking at you to fix it. What do you do?

You're someone who stays composed under pressure and handles crises with steady calm. Your car breaks down on a dark highway at night with no cell service and your kids are in the back seat. How do you respond?
